paper_id.description: Unique ID for the paper or case. Copy this from the input schema.

reference_id.description: Unique ID for the planned analysis path used as input. If the input uses planned_id instead, copy that value here.

reanalyst_id.description: ID associated with the planned analysis path, if provided. Use not_stated if not provided.

focal_claim.description: The focal claim tested by the executed analysis. Copy this from the input schema.
focal_claim.example: The likelihood of decision error should decrease with the degree of risk aversion.

overall_execution_status.description: Overall execution status across all attempted tasks.

target_task_ids.description: List of task IDs the agent attempted, copied from the input schema.

completed_task_ids.description: List of task IDs that produced usable outputs.

failed_task_ids.description: List of task IDs that failed. Use an empty list if none failed.

overall_summary.description: Short summary of what the agent executed and what result it obtained.
overall_summary.example: The agent executed both planned tasks and found a negative association between risk aversion and decision error.

task_id.description: Task ID for this output entry.

task_role.description: Role of the task, copied from the input schema.

execution_status.description: Execution status for this specific task.

path_name.description: Name of the analysis path that was actually executed.

software.description: Software or language actually used to run the task.

model_family.description: Model or test family actually executed.

executed_files.description: Scripts or files actually executed for this task.

run_command.description: Command actually used to run the task.

code_source.description: Source of the executed analysis code.

followed_planned_path.description: Whether the execution followed the planned analysis path.

deviation_type.description: Type of change made during execution.

description.description: What changed from the planned path or code.
description.example: Updated the data file path so the script could locate RiskData.dta.

reason.example: The original script used a local path that did not exist in the container.

likely_impact.description: Likely impact of the deviation on the result.

fidelity_note.description: Short judgment of whether the agent followed the planned analysis path.
fidelity_note.example: The agent followed the planned path; only a file path was adjusted.

metric.description: Raw result metric produced by the task.

value.description: Main raw numeric result value. Use null if unavailable.

direction.description: Direction of the result relative to the focal claim or focal coefficient.

t_value.description: t statistic if available; otherwise null.

f_value.description: F statistic if available; otherwise null.

z_value.description: z statistic if available; otherwise null.

standard_error.description: Standard error if available; otherwise null.

confidence_interval.description: Confidence interval if available; otherwise null.

sample_size.description: Sample size used in the executed analysis if available; otherwise null.

other.description: Any other relevant statistic not covered above.

result_text.description: Short plain-language statement of the raw result.
result_text.example: Higher risk aversion was associated with fewer decision errors.

conclusion_class.description: Whether this task result supports the focal claim.

conclusion_text.example: The result supports the focal claim because higher risk aversion is associated with fewer decision errors.

output_files.description: Generated files such as result JSON, tables, logs, or figures.

failure_reason.description: Reason for failure, or null if successful.

failure_stage.description: Where the failure occurred, or null if successful.

repair_attempts_used.description: Number of bounded repair attempts used for this task.
